data.title: "Jun Ji-hyun's husband lost 15 billion won in cryptocurrency trading" became a hot search on Weibo and Baidu
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News "Jun Ji-hyun's husband lost 15 billion won in cryptocurrency trading" has been on Weibo and Baidu hot searches, currently ranking 15th on Baidu's hot search list and 21st on Weibo's hot search list.
Earlier, Korean media Chosun revealed that Choi Joon Hyuk (최준혁, Choi Joon Hyuk), the husband of the famous Korean star Jun Ji-hyun, is the CEO of Alpha Asset Management. The private equity fund operator holds 35 billion won worth of shares in the listed company Wemade. The latter's virtual currency Wemix issued through its subsidiary has been removed from Korean cryptocurrency exchanges such as Upbit and Bithumb, and Wemade's stock price has also fallen sharply.

data.title: Singapore to Block Unlicensed Trading Platforms Octa and XM
data.description:
According to PANews, Singapore's Monetary Authority and police plan to block access to the unlicensed trading platforms Octa and XM starting June 20. Once the block is in effect, users will be unable to access these websites through Singaporean internet service providers.

In a joint statement, the Monetary Authority of Singapore and the police highlighted that both platforms have been offering and promoting trading services, such as leveraged forex trading, commodities, indices, and stocks, to local residents without the necessary licenses and qualifications. This activity violates the Securities and Futures Act of 2001. Additionally, both platforms engage in cryptocurrency trading.

The authorities noted that most unregulated online trading platforms are based overseas, making it difficult to verify their operational credibility and posing a high risk of fraud. Octa is operated by Octa Markets and Uni Fin Invest, while XM is run by XM Global.

data.title: Uber CEO: Bitcoin is a proven commodity, stablecoins can facilitate international transactions

data.title: Wuhan police smashed a fraud den that was suspected of inducing "netizens" to recharge virtual currency on a fake platform
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Guangming.com, Wuhan Dongxihu police recently destroyed a fraud den hidden in the countryside, arrested 32 criminal suspects, and 20 people were criminally detained by the police. After interrogation, the fraud gang pretended to fabricate a "white, rich and beautiful" dating script, boasted about the investment income of virtual currency, and then induced victims to invest in the fake platform they created. The scammers used the carefully fabricated "script" to show concern for the victims to get closer, and then boasted about the "investment" income. Once the victim is tempted, the suspect will send a fake investment website to induce the victim to buy virtual currency and invest in the fake platform. The income is controllable by the scammers, but the "cash withdrawal" cannot be successful. At present, the case is under further processing.
